name: Datadog

auth.notes: Datadog also supports API key + application key authentication (header-based: DD-API-KEY and DD-APPLICATION-KEY). OAuth2 is used for Datadog marketplace integrations. For direct API access, API/app keys are the primary authentication method. Access tokens have a 1-hour TTL. Regional API domains: api.datadoghq.com (US1), api.datadoghq.eu (EU), api.us3.datadoghq.com (US3), api.us5.datadoghq.com (US5), api.ddog-gov.com (US1-FED), api.ap1.datadoghq.com (AP1).

api.rateLimits: Varies by endpoint. Response headers include X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset, X-RateLimit-Period. Returns HTTP 429 when exceeded. Can be increased by contacting Datadog support. Example limits: Events submission 250,000/min, Graph snapshot 60/hour, Log query 300/hour, Metric retrieval 100/hour.

scim.notes: SCIM provisioning is available on Infrastructure Pro and Infrastructure Enterprise plans. Supports user provisioning via /Users endpoint. Team provisioning via SCIM is in limited availability (contact Datadog support). Regional SCIM endpoints follow regional API domains. Supports Okta and Microsoft Entra ID as identity providers. Use a service account application key for SCIM configuration.

webhooks.registrationEndpoint: Configured via Datadog Webhooks integration tile or POST /api/v1/integration/webhooks/configuration/webhooks
webhooks.notes: Datadog webhooks are triggered by monitor alerts and notifications, not by user/IAM lifecycle events. The webhook sends a POST request with JSON payload containing event details. Payloads include fields: host, title, body, alert_metric, alert_status, alert_transition, tags, event_type, priority, link, date, email, org. Retry: up to 5 retries on 5XX responses, 15-second timeout per request.

sandbox.notes: Datadog offers a 14-day free trial of the Pro plan. Free tier includes limited monitoring for up to 5 hosts. Trial includes full access to all features including user management APIs.

needs_verification: Exact rate limits for user management endpoints (not documented separately from general rate limits)
